headline: Sedition Act used 23 times since reform promise — only against opposition
context: The Sedition Act 1948, which the government promised to repeal in 2018, has been used 23 times since 2022 — all against opposition politicians, activists, and journalists.

cards.big: Sedition Act used 23 times since 2022 — exclusively against critics, never allies
cards.sub: Colonial-era law survives every reform promise and targets only one direction

cards.big: PH manifesto 2018 Item 27 promised Sedition Act repeal within first year
cards.sub: Eight years later, the law not only survives but is actively deployed

cards.big: 23 prosecutions cost estimated RM4.6 million in AGC resources and court time
cards.sub: Public funds used to suppress speech while real crimes face prosecution backlogs

cards.big: Zero government-aligned figures have been charged under Sedition Act since 2022
cards.sub: Selective prosecution pattern reveals law is a political tool, not a legal principle

cards.big: A sedition law used only against critics isn't protecting stability — it's protecting power
Should the Sedition Act be repealed and replaced with proportionate public order legislation?
